mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate increases the present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return hit rate is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 10%, it signals severe demand destruction and corporate revenue collapse, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 10%, or Rank IC is greater than -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in CPIAUCSL combined with a low federal funds rate signals negative real rates, forcing investors into equities and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of observations when CPIAUCSL is rising and FEDFUNDS is low, or interaction term t-statistic falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery, a month-over-month rebound in retail sales signals recovering consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 55% of months when RSAFS rebounds during identified recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates contracting manufacturing activity and corporate earnings pressure, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day return hit rate is less than 55% during INDPRO decline episodes, or Rank IC exceeds -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll declines anticipate broader economic contraction and reduced consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls by more than 1% over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered payroll decline episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales confirm broad demand collapse, amplifying negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
failure_condition: Joint negative signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or interaction term F-statistic p-value exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating inflation reading above 3% annualized forces expectations of tighter monetary policy ahead, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 55% of triggered high-inflation episodes.
